sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: EHA: J&J sharpens myeloma edge as Talvey, Darzalex Faspro combo proves its worth in earlier disease stage
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson presented positive Phase 3 results for its Talvey and Darzalex Faspro combination therapy in earlier-stage multiple myeloma at the European Hematology Association (EHA) annual meeting. The regimen significantly reduced the risk of disease progression or death by up to 72% and death by up to 53% compared to standard therapy. This win marks the first time a GPRC5D bispecific antibody combo has shown superior progression-free survival in earlier-line multiple myeloma, leading J&J to pursue regulatory approval for earlier use.

feed.title: Analysts Conflicted on These Technology Names: Microsoft (MSFT) and Paychex (PAYX)
feed.summary: Analysts are divided on two technology giants: Microsoft (MSFT) and Paychex (PAYX). Wells Fargo maintained a "Buy" rating for Microsoft with a $650 price target, aligning with a "Strong Buy" consensus, while reiterating a "Sell" rating for Paychex with a $95 price target, contrasting with an overall "Hold" consensus. This report highlights the differing analyst perspectives on these companies' stock performance.

feed.title: Lymphoma drug combo cut risk of progression or death 25%
feed.summary: Incyte announced positive results from its pivotal Phase 3 frontMIND trial, showing that the tafasitamab (Monjuvi/Minjuvi) combination therapy significantly prolonged progression-free survival in patients with previously untreated high-risk diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) or high-grade B-cell lymphoma (HGBL). The treatment reduced the risk of disease progression or death by 25% and was generally well-tolerated. These results support global regulatory applications for the therapy as a potential new standard of care.

feed.title: Is Rackspace (RXT) Using Its Riyadh HQ to Redefine Its Sovereign Cloud Edge?
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology has opened a new regional headquarters in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, to focus on sovereign cloud capabilities for enterprise, government, and regulated industries in the Gulf Cooperation Council. This move supports Rackspace's strategy to pivot from legacy cloud services to higher-value AI, security, and sovereign cloud offerings, tying into its recent agreement with AMD for an Enterprise AI Cloud. While this strengthens its regulated cloud solutions, the company still faces near-term challenges from revenue declines and negative free cash flow.

feed.title: Why SPX Technologies Stock’s Income Statement Tells a Different Story Than the Start-Up Cost Headlines in 2026
feed.summary: SPX Technologies (SPXC) exceeded Q1 2026 estimates, driven by a 17% year-over-year revenue increase and a significant acceleration in data center cooling demand, leading to raised full-year guidance. Despite temporary start-up costs impacting HVAC segment margins, the company demonstrates strong operating leverage and maintains a substantial operating margin advantage over competitors like Rexnord and Watts Water. TIKR's mid-case valuation suggests a 47% total return by December 2034, indicating the market may not yet fully price in the benefits of SPXC's capacity expansion and data center revenue ramp.
